svg.text: tri-consonantal roots · verb conjugation · dual number · broken plurals · Hebrew cognates
— · — THE TRI-CONSONANTAL ROOT · SAME SYSTEM AS HEBREW — · —
Arabic and Hebrew both build vocabulary from 3-letter roots · same ancestral Semitic system
ك-ت-ب K-T-B · WRITING · same root as Hebrew כ-ת-ב
Arabic verb: root + pattern · suffix marks person/gender in past · prefix/suffix in present
Arabic has THREE numbers: singular, dual, plural · dual adds -ayn/-an suffix
BROKEN PLURALS: Arabic plurals often change the internal vowel pattern (not just add a suffix)
same Proto-Semitic ancestor · many shared roots · different but mutually illuminating
